- <nextTask>completed</nextTask> - Mark current task completed, get next task
- <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> - Mark current task blocked, explain why
- <LogProgress><taskId>task_id</taskId><whatWasDone>What you did</whatWasDone></LogProgress> - Log progress on current task
- <viewTask/> - View current plan and tasks
- <listTasks/> - List all tasks
- <jobDone/> - Finish the plan (only when all tasks are done or you want to end early)
- <createPlan><title>Plan Title</title><instructions>Goal description</instructions></createPlan> - Create a new plan (replaces current). Use when current plan needs full replacement.
- <createTask><title>Task Title</title><instruction>What to do</instruction></createTask> - Add a new task to the current plan. Always create a plan first.
- <updateTask><taskId>id</taskId><title>New Title</title><instruction>New instruction</instruction></updateTask> - Update a task's title and/or instruction.
- <deleteTask><taskId>id</taskId></deleteTask> - Remove a task from the current plan.
- <deletePlan/> - Delete the current plan entirely.
- <deleteDraft/> - Delete the unsaved draft plan.
- <deleteAllPlans/> - Delete all plans.

TOOL USAGE RULES:
- NEVER call multiple tool calls for large content. Split large data: WriteFile first chunk -> AppendFile remaining.
- Prefer targeted edits: Use ReplaceLine for specific line changes and AppendFile for additions instead of rewriting entire files with WriteFile. This is more precise and preserves unrelated content.
- Prefer XML tools (Grep, Find, List) over Terminal commands (grep, find, ls).
- For file manipulation with complex data, use WriteFile/AppendFile. For one-liners, echo/cat/tee with Terminal is fine.
- Use ExecuteScript to run scripts you create (WriteFile/CreateFile). Terminal is for system binaries only.
- Save useful commands and solutions as tips with <SaveTip>. Retrieve them with <GetTip>. Browse with <ListTips>. Bring saved tips into context with <ReinsertTip>.
- XML Content: Never use backslashes to escape characters inside XML values вҖ” the parser handles special characters natively. Write raw content without escaping quotes (write `"Hello"` not `"Hello"`).

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (tasks from plan mode):
1. Task received: "Create project folder with basic files"
2. Use Terminal to create folder: mkdir myproject
3. Use WriteFile/CreateFile to create index.html, style.css, app.js
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>1</taskId><whatWasDone>Created project folder with 3 files</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Read back files with ReadFile to verify content is correct
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
7. Next task is received automatically
8. Repeat until all tasks done
9. Call <jobDone/> when finished

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (self-created tasks in build mode):
1. Create plan and tasks with createPlan + createTask
2. Call <planDone/> to start the first pending task
3. Execute the task using available tools
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>What was accomplished</whatWasDone></LogProgress>

6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask> when done

8. Call <jobDone/> when finished

[Tool Result: ReadFile] #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~5 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input, ScrollableContainer
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 1fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 5;
        border: solid blue;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~5 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        current = output_area.renderable or ""
        output_area.update(current + message)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

- <Terminal><arg1>ls</arg1><arg2>-l</arg2></Terminal>: Execute terminal commands. Use ONLY for one-liner commands. Params: <arg1>, [<arg2>], ... (dynamic args)
- <ReadFile><fileName>README.md</fileName></ReadFile>: Read file. Params: <fileName>
- <WriteFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Simple hello world app.</contentOfFile></WriteFile>: Write file. Use for content < 4096 bytes. For larger content, use WriteFile for first chunk then AppendFile. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <AppendFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Second line</contentOfFile><fromLineNumber>1</fromLineNumber></AppendFile>: Append to file. Use for content > 4096 bytes or adding to existing files. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>, [<fromLineNumber>]
- <CreateFile><fileName>testfile.sh</fileName><contentOfFile># new content</contentOfFile></CreateFile>: Create new file (fails if exists). If file exists and you want to overwrite, use WriteFile instead. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <List><path>.</path></List>: List files. Prefer this over Terminal ls. Params: [<path>] (optional)
- <listTools/>: Show all tools. No params.
- <TreeView><path>.</path><depth>3</depth></TreeView>: Show directory tree. Explore project structure, set depth=0 for unlimited. Params: [<path>], [<depth>] (default 3), [<pattern>] (glob filter), [<showHidden>]
- <ExecuteScript><fileName>ls</fileName><args>-l</args></ExecuteScript>: Run script (.py, .sh, .js, etc). Params: <fileName>, [<args>]
- <Grep><pattern>search_term</pattern><fileName>file.txt</fileName><recursive>true</recursive></Grep>: Search by regex. Prefer this over Terminal grep. Params: <pattern>, [<fileName>], [<recursive>]
- <Diff><file1>file1.txt</file1><file2>file2.txt</file2><unified>3</unified></Diff>: Compare files. Params: <file1>, <file2>, [<unified>]
- <Sed><pattern>old_text</pattern><replacement>new_text</replacement><fileName>file.txt</fileName><inplace>true</inplace></Sed>: Find/replace. Params: <pattern>, <replacement>, <fileName>, [<inplace>]
- <ReplaceLine><fileName>file.txt</fileName><fromLine>10</fromLine><toLine>20</toLine><replacement>new content</replacement></ReplaceLine>: Replace specific line(s) in a file. Use for targeted edits instead of rewriting the whole file. Params: <fileName>, <fromLine> (required), [<toLine>] (optional, defaults to fromLine), <replacement>
- <Find><pattern>*.py</pattern><path>.</path></Find>: Find by name. Prefer this over Terminal find. Params: <pattern>, [<path>]
- <Head><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Head>: First N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Tail><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Tail>: Last N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Sort><fileName>file.txt</fileName><numeric>true</numeric></Sort>: Sort lines. Params: <fileName>, [<numeric>], [<reverse>], [<unique>]

- <nextTask>completed</nextTask> - Mark current task completed, get next task
- <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> - Mark current task blocked, explain why
- <LogProgress><taskId>task_id</taskId><whatWasDone>What you did</whatWasDone></LogProgress> - Log progress on current task
- <viewTask/> - View current plan and tasks
- <listTasks/> - List all tasks
- <jobDone/> - Finish the plan (only when all tasks are done or you want to end early)
- <createPlan><title>Plan Title</title><instructions>Goal description</instructions></createPlan> - Create a new plan (replaces current). Use when current plan needs full replacement.
- <createTask><title>Task Title</title><instruction>What to do</instruction></createTask> - Add a new task to the current plan. Always create a plan first.
- <updateTask><taskId>id</taskId><title>New Title</title><instruction>New instruction</instruction></updateTask> - Update a task's title and/or instruction.
- <deleteTask><taskId>id</taskId></deleteTask> - Remove a task from the current plan.
- <deletePlan/> - Delete the current plan entirely.
- <deleteDraft/> - Delete the unsaved draft plan.
- <deleteAllPlans/> - Delete all plans.

TOOL USAGE RULES:
- NEVER call multiple tool calls for large content. Split large data: WriteFile first chunk -> AppendFile remaining.
- Prefer targeted edits: Use ReplaceLine for specific line changes and AppendFile for additions instead of rewriting entire files with WriteFile. This is more precise and preserves unrelated content.
- Prefer XML tools (Grep, Find, List) over Terminal commands (grep, find, ls).
- For file manipulation with complex data, use WriteFile/AppendFile. For one-liners, echo/cat/tee with Terminal is fine.
- Use ExecuteScript to run scripts you create (WriteFile/CreateFile). Terminal is for system binaries only.
- Save useful commands and solutions as tips with <SaveTip>. Retrieve them with <GetTip>. Browse with <ListTips>. Bring saved tips into context with <ReinsertTip>.
- XML Content: Never use backslashes to escape characters inside XML values вҖ” the parser handles special characters natively. Write raw content without escaping quotes (write `"Hello"` not `"Hello"`).

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (tasks from plan mode):
1. Task received: "Create project folder with basic files"
2. Use Terminal to create folder: mkdir myproject
3. Use WriteFile/CreateFile to create index.html, style.css, app.js
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>1</taskId><whatWasDone>Created project folder with 3 files</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Read back files with ReadFile to verify content is correct
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
7. Next task is received automatically
8. Repeat until all tasks done
9. Call <jobDone/> when finished

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (self-created tasks in build mode):
1. Create plan and tasks with createPlan + createTask
2. Call <planDone/> to start the first pending task
3. Execute the task using available tools
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>What was accomplished</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Verify work with ReadFile/List/TreeView
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask> when done
7. Repeat until all tasks done
8. Call <jobDone/> when finished

BLOCKED EXAMPLE:
1. Task: "Install dependency X"
2. Attempt: <Terminal><arg1>pip</arg1><arg2>install</arg2><arg3>package-that-does-not-exist</arg3></Terminal>
3. Error: "Package not found"
4. <LogProgress><taskId>3</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted to install package-that-does-not-exist, failed with error: Package not found</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. <nextTask>blocked</nextTask>: "Tried to install package-that-does-not-exist via pip but got error 'Package not found'. Need to verify correct package name or install from alternative source. User should provide correct package name or installation method."

I'll analyze this new error and fix it. The issue is that `Static` widgets in Textual don't have a `renderable` attribute to retrieve their current content. Let me fix this by properly using the reactive variable that's already defined.
[Tool Result: ReadFile] #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~5 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 1fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 5;
        border: solid blue;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~5 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        current = output_area.renderable or ""
        output_area.update(current + message)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

<createTask><title>Fix Static widget content handling</title><instruction>The error occurs because Static widgets don't have a 'renderable' attribute. The code has a reactive variable `output_text` defined but it's not being used.

Fix the code to properly use the reactive variable:
1. Remove the line that tries to access `output_area.renderable`
2. Instead, append to `self.output_text` (the reactive variable)
3. Watch the reactive variable to update the Static widget when it changes

Changes needed:
- In `update_output()`: Remove `current = output_area.renderable or ""` and instead use `self.output_text += message`
- Add a `watch` method to update the Static widget when `output_text` changes
- For the "clear" command, set `self.output_text = ""` instead of calling `.update("")` directly

Alternative simpler fix: Just track output in a regular instance variable since we already have the reactive variable defined but not used properly.

The simplest fix:
1. In `update_output()`: Change to use `self.output_text += message` and then `output_area.update(self.output_text)`
2. In `on_input_submitted()` for clear command: Change to `self.output_text = ""` and `self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")`</instruction></createTask>

[Tool Result: ReadFile] #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~5 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 1fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 5;
        border: solid blue;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~5 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        self.output_text += message
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        output_area.update(self.output_text)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.output_text = ""
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

3. MANDATORY PROGRESS REPORTING: After completing any meaningful step, call <LogProgress><taskId>TASK_ID</taskId><whatWasDone>Specific action completed</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
4. MANDATORY SELF-VERIFICATION: Before calling <nextTask>completed</nextTask>, verify your work:
   - If you wrote code: verify it runs or read it back with ReadFile
   - If you made edits: verify changes with ReadFile
   - If you created files: confirm they exist with List or TreeView
   - If you deleted files: confirm they no longer exist
5. When a task is verified completed, call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
6. If blocked, call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> with detailed explanation
7. When all tasks done, call <jobDone/> to finish the plan

BLOCKED TASK HANDLING:
When you encounter a blocker:
1. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted X, blocked because Y</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
2. Call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> and explain EXACTLY:
   - What you tried to do
   - What blocked you (error message, missing file, etc.)
   - What specific information or action is needed to unblock
   - What the user should do or provide

AVAILABLE TOOLS (use exact XML format):
- <Terminal><arg1>ls</arg1><arg2>-l</arg2></Terminal>: Execute terminal commands. Use ONLY for one-liner commands. Params: <arg1>, [<arg2>], ... (dynamic args)
- <ReadFile><fileName>README.md</fileName></ReadFile>: Read file. Params: <fileName>
- <WriteFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Simple hello world app.</contentOfFile></WriteFile>: Write file. Use for content < 4096 bytes. For larger content, use WriteFile for first chunk then AppendFile. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <AppendFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Second line</contentOfFile><fromLineNumber>1</fromLineNumber></AppendFile>: Append to file. Use for content > 4096 bytes or adding to existing files. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>, [<fromLineNumber>]
- <CreateFile><fileName>testfile.sh</fileName><contentOfFile># new content</contentOfFile></CreateFile>: Create new file (fails if exists). If file exists and you want to overwrite, use WriteFile instead. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <List><path>.</path></List>: List files. Prefer this over Terminal ls. Params: [<path>] (optional)
- <listTools/>: Show all tools. No params.
- <TreeView><path>.</path><depth>3</depth></TreeView>: Show directory tree. Explore project structure, set depth=0 for unlimited. Params: [<path>], [<depth>] (default 3), [<pattern>] (glob filter), [<showHidden>]
- <ExecuteScript><fileName>ls</fileName><args>-l</args></ExecuteScript>: Run script (.py, .sh, .js, etc). Params: <fileName>, [<args>]
- <Grep><pattern>search_term</pattern><fileName>file.txt</fileName><recursive>true</recursive></Grep>: Search by regex. Prefer this over Terminal grep. Params: <pattern>, [<fileName>], [<recursive>]
- <Diff><file1>file1.txt</file1><file2>file2.txt</file2><unified>3</unified></Diff>: Compare files. Params: <file1>, <file2>, [<unified>]
- <Sed><pattern>old_text</pattern><replacement>new_text</replacement><fileName>file.txt</fileName><inplace>true</inplace></Sed>: Find/replace. Params: <pattern>, <replacement>, <fileName>, [<inplace>]
- <ReplaceLine><fileName>file.txt</fileName><fromLine>10</fromLine><toLine>20</toLine><replacement>new content</replacement></ReplaceLine>: Replace specific line(s) in a file. Use for targeted edits instead of rewriting the whole file. Params: <fileName>, <fromLine> (required), [<toLine>] (optional, defaults to fromLine), <replacement>
- <Find><pattern>*.py</pattern><path>.</path></Find>: Find by name. Prefer this over Terminal find. Params: <pattern>, [<path>]
- <Head><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Head>: First N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Tail><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Tail>: Last N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Sort><fileName>file.txt</fileName><numeric>true</numeric></Sort>: Sort lines. Params: <fileName>, [<numeric>], [<reverse>], [<unique>]

PLAN MANAGEMENT TOOLS (use these to track progress):
- <planDone/> - Signal planning is done, start the first pending task
- <nextTask>completed</nextTask> - Mark current task completed, get next task
- <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> - Mark current task blocked, explain why
- <LogProgress><taskId>task_id</taskId><whatWasDone>What you did</whatWasDone></LogProgress> - Log progress on current task
- <viewTask/> - View current plan and tasks
- <listTasks/> - List all tasks
- <jobDone/> - Finish the plan (only when all tasks are done or you want to end early)
- <createPlan><title>Plan Title</title><instructions>Goal description</instructions></createPlan> - Create a new plan (replaces current). Use when current plan needs full replacement.
- <createTask><title>Task Title</title><instruction>What to do</instruction></createTask> - Add a new task to the current plan. Always create a plan first.
- <updateTask><taskId>id</taskId><title>New Title</title><instruction>New instruction</instruction></updateTask> - Update a task's title and/or instruction.
- <deleteTask><taskId>id</taskId></deleteTask> - Remove a task from the current plan.
- <deletePlan/> - Delete the current plan entirely.
- <deleteDraft/> - Delete the unsaved draft plan.
- <deleteAllPlans/> - Delete all plans.

TOOL USAGE RULES:
- NEVER call multiple tool calls for large content. Split large data: WriteFile first chunk -> AppendFile remaining.
- Prefer targeted edits: Use ReplaceLine for specific line changes and AppendFile for additions instead of rewriting entire files with WriteFile. This is more precise and preserves unrelated content.
- Prefer XML tools (Grep, Find, List) over Terminal commands (grep, find, ls).
- For file manipulation with complex data, use WriteFile/AppendFile. For one-liners, echo/cat/tee with Terminal is fine.
- Use ExecuteScript to run scripts you create (WriteFile/CreateFile). Terminal is for system binaries only.
- Save useful commands and solutions as tips with <SaveTip>. Retrieve them with <GetTip>. Browse with <ListTips>. Bring saved tips into context with <ReinsertTip>.
- XML Content: Never use backslashes to escape characters inside XML values вҖ” the parser handles special characters natively. Write raw content without escaping quotes (write `"Hello"` not `"Hello"`).

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (tasks from plan mode):
1. Task received: "Create project folder with basic files"
2. Use Terminal to create folder: mkdir myproject
3. Use WriteFile/CreateFile to create index.html, style.css, app.js
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>1</taskId><whatWasDone>Created project folder with 3 files</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Read back files with ReadFile to verify content is correct
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
7. Next task is received automatically
8. Repeat until all tasks done
9. Call <jobDone/> when finished

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (self-created tasks in build mode):
1. Create plan and tasks with createPlan + createTask
2. Call <planDone/> to start the first pending task
3. Execute the task using available tools
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>What was accomplished</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Verify work with ReadFile/List/TreeView
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask> when done
7. Repeat until all tasks done
8. Call <jobDone/> when finished

BLOCKED EXAMPLE:
1. Task: "Install dependency X"
2. Attempt: <Terminal><arg1>pip</arg1><arg2>install</arg2><arg3>package-that-does-not-exist</arg3></Terminal>
3. Error: "Package not found"
4. <LogProgress><taskId>3</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted to install package-that-does-not-exist, failed with error: Package not found</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. <nextTask>blocked</nextTask>: "Tried to install package-that-does-not-exist via pip but got error 'Package not found'. Need to verify correct package name or install from alternative source. User should provide correct package name or installation method."

[Tool Result: ReadFile] #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~5 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 1fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    #input-container {
        height: 3;
        border: solid blue;
    }
    }
        border: solid blue;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~5 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        self.output_text += message
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        output_area.update(self.output_text)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.output_text = ""
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

- <LogProgress><taskId>task_id</taskId><whatWasDone>What you did</whatWasDone></LogProgress> - Log progress on current task
- <viewTask/> - View current plan and tasks
- <listTasks/> - List all tasks
- <jobDone/> - Finish the plan (only when all tasks are done or you want to end early)
- <createPlan><title>Plan Title</title><instructions>Goal description</instructions></createPlan> - Create a new plan (replaces current). Use when current plan needs full replacement.
- <createTask><title>Task Title</title><instruction>What to do</instruction></createTask> - Add a new task to the current plan. Always create a plan first.
- <updateTask><taskId>id</taskId><title>New Title</title><instruction>New instruction</instruction></updateTask> - Update a task's title and/or instruction.
- <deleteTask><taskId>id</taskId></deleteTask> - Remove a task from the current plan.
- <deletePlan/> - Delete the current plan entirely.
- <deleteDraft/> - Delete the unsaved draft plan.
- <deleteAllPlans/> - Delete all plans.

TOOL USAGE RULES:
- NEVER call multiple tool calls for large content. Split large data: WriteFile first chunk -> AppendFile remaining.
- Prefer targeted edits: Use ReplaceLine for specific line changes and AppendFile for additions instead of rewriting entire files with WriteFile. This is more precise and preserves unrelated content.
- Prefer XML tools (Grep, Find, List) over Terminal commands (grep, find, ls).
- For file manipulation with complex data, use WriteFile/AppendFile. For one-liners, echo/cat/tee with Terminal is fine.
- Use ExecuteScript to run scripts you create (WriteFile/CreateFile). Terminal is for system binaries only.
- Save useful commands and solutions as tips with <SaveTip>. Retrieve them with <GetTip>. Browse with <ListTips>. Bring saved tips into context with <ReinsertTip>.
- XML Content: Never use backslashes to escape characters inside XML values вҖ” the parser handles special characters natively. Write raw content without escaping quotes (write `"Hello"` not `"Hello"`).

1. Task received: "Create project folder with basic files"
2. Use Terminal to create folder: mkdir myproject
3. Use WriteFile/CreateFile to create index.html, style.css, app.js
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>1</taskId><whatWasDone>Created project folder with 3 files</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Read back files with ReadFile to verify content is correct
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
7. Next task is received automatically
8. Repeat until all tasks done

[Tool Result: ReadFile] #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~3 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 1fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 3;
        border: solid blue;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~3 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        self.output_text += message
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        output_area.update(self.output_text)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.output_text = ""
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

You are in BUILD MODE. You are code agent. Your role is to execute the tasks created in plan mode.

MODE: BUILD (Thinking ENABLED - you can reason step by step)

IMPORTANT WORKFLOW:
1. You will receive tasks automatically (from plan mode). Execute each task using available tools.
2. If you created your own tasks in build mode, call <planDone/> to start executing the first task.
3. MANDATORY PROGRESS REPORTING: After completing any meaningful step, call <LogProgress><taskId>TASK_ID</taskId><whatWasDone>Specific action completed</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
4. MANDATORY SELF-VERIFICATION: Before calling <nextTask>completed</nextTask>, verify your work:
   - If you wrote code: verify it runs or read it back with ReadFile
   - If you made edits: verify changes with ReadFile
   - If you created files: confirm they exist with List or TreeView
   - If you deleted files: confirm they no longer exist
5. When a task is verified completed, call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
6. If blocked, call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> with detailed explanation
7. When all tasks done, call <jobDone/> to finish the plan

BLOCKED TASK HANDLING:
When you encounter a blocker:
1. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted X, blocked because Y</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
2. Call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> and explain EXACTLY:
   - What you tried to do
   - What blocked you (error message, missing file, etc.)
   - What specific information or action is needed to unblock
   - What the user should do or provide

AVAILABLE TOOLS (use exact XML format):
- <Terminal><arg1>ls</arg1><arg2>-l</arg2></Terminal>: Execute terminal commands. Use ONLY for one-liner commands. Params: <arg1>, [<arg2>], ... (dynamic args)
- <ReadFile><fileName>README.md</fileName></ReadFile>: Read file. Params: <fileName>
- <WriteFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Simple hello world app.</contentOfFile></WriteFile>: Write file. Use for content < 4096 bytes. For larger content, use WriteFile for first chunk then AppendFile. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <AppendFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Second line</contentOfFile><fromLineNumber>1</fromLineNumber></AppendFile>: Append to file. Use for content > 4096 bytes or adding to existing files. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>, [<fromLineNumber>]
- <CreateFile><fileName>testfile.sh</fileName><contentOfFile># new content</contentOfFile></CreateFile>: Create new file (fails if exists). If file exists and you want to overwrite, use WriteFile instead. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <List><path>.</path></List>: List files. Prefer this over Terminal ls. Params: [<path>] (optional)
- <listTools/>: Show all tools. No params.
- <TreeView><path>.</path><depth>3</depth></TreeView>: Show directory tree. Explore project structure, set depth=0 for unlimited. Params: [<path>], [<depth>] (default 3), [<pattern>] (glob filter), [<showHidden>]
- <ExecuteScript><fileName>ls</fileName><args>-l</args></ExecuteScript>: Run script (.py, .sh, .js, etc). Params: <fileName>, [<args>]
- <Grep><pattern>search_term</pattern><fileName>file.txt</fileName><recursive>true</recursive></Grep>: Search by regex. Prefer this over Terminal grep. Params: <pattern>, [<fileName>], [<recursive>]
- <Diff><file1>file1.txt</file1><file2>file2.txt</file2><unified>3</unified></Diff>: Compare files. Params: <file1>, <file2>, [<unified>]
- <Sed><pattern>old_text</pattern><replacement>new_text</replacement><fileName>file.txt</fileName><inplace>true</inplace></Sed>: Find/replace. Params: <pattern>, <replacement>, <fileName>, [<inplace>]
- <ReplaceLine><fileName>file.txt</fileName><fromLine>10</fromLine><toLine>20</toLine><replacement>new content</replacement></ReplaceLine>: Replace specific line(s) in a file. Use for targeted edits instead of rewriting the whole file. Params: <fileName>, <fromLine> (required), [<toLine>] (optional, defaults to fromLine), <replacement>
- <Find><pattern>*.py</pattern><path>.</path></Find>: Find by name. Prefer this over Terminal find. Params: <pattern>, [<path>]
- <Head><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Head>: First N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Tail><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Tail>: Last N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Sort><fileName>file.txt</fileName><numeric>true</numeric></Sort>: Sort lines. Params: <fileName>, [<numeric>], [<reverse>], [<unique>]

[Tool Result: ReadFile] #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~3 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 1fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 3;
        border: solid blue;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~3 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        self.output_text += message
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        output_area.update(self.output_text)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.output_text = ""
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

   - What specific information or action is needed to unblock
   - What the user should do or provide

AVAILABLE TOOLS (use exact XML format):
- <Terminal><arg1>ls</arg1><arg2>-l</arg2></Terminal>: Execute terminal commands. Use ONLY for one-liner commands. Params: <arg1>, [<arg2>], ... (dynamic args)
- <ReadFile><fileName>README.md</fileName></ReadFile>: Read file. Params: <fileName>
- <WriteFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Simple hello world app.</contentOfFile></WriteFile>: Write file. Use for content < 4096 bytes. For larger content, use WriteFile for first chunk then AppendFile. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <AppendFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Second line</contentOfFile><fromLineNumber>1</fromLineNumber></AppendFile>: Append to file. Use for content > 4096 bytes or adding to existing files. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>, [<fromLineNumber>]
- <CreateFile><fileName>testfile.sh</fileName><contentOfFile># new content</contentOfFile></CreateFile>: Create new file (fails if exists). If file exists and you want to overwrite, use WriteFile instead. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <List><path>.</path></List>: List files. Prefer this over Terminal ls. Params: [<path>] (optional)
- <listTools/>: Show all tools. No params.
- <TreeView><path>.</path><depth>3</depth></TreeView>: Show directory tree. Explore project structure, set depth=0 for unlimited. Params: [<path>], [<depth>] (default 3), [<pattern>] (glob filter), [<showHidden>]
- <ExecuteScript><fileName>ls</fileName><args>-l</args></ExecuteScript>: Run script (.py, .sh, .js, etc). Params: <fileName>, [<args>]
- <Grep><pattern>search_term</pattern><fileName>file.txt</fileName><recursive>true</recursive></Grep>: Search by regex. Prefer this over Terminal grep. Params: <pattern>, [<fileName>], [<recursive>]
- <Diff><file1>file1.txt</file1><file2>file2.txt</file2><unified>3</unified></Diff>: Compare files. Params: <file1>, <file2>, [<unified>]
- <Sed><pattern>old_text</pattern><replacement>new_text</replacement><fileName>file.txt</fileName><inplace>true</inplace></Sed>: Find/replace. Params: <pattern>, <replacement>, <fileName>, [<inplace>]
- <ReplaceLine><fileName>file.txt</fileName><fromLine>10</fromLine><toLine>20</toLine><replacement>new content</replacement></ReplaceLine>: Replace specific line(s) in a file. Use for targeted edits instead of rewriting the whole file. Params: <fileName>, <fromLine> (required), [<toLine>] (optional, defaults to fromLine), <replacement>
- <Find><pattern>*.py</pattern><path>.</path></Find>: Find by name. Prefer this over Terminal find. Params: <pattern>, [<path>]
- <Head><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Head>: First N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Tail><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Tail>: Last N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Sort><fileName>file.txt</fileName><numeric>true</numeric></Sort>: Sort lines. Params: <fileName>, [<numeric>], [<reverse>], [<unique>]

PLAN MANAGEMENT TOOLS (use these to track progress):
- <planDone/> - Signal planning is done, start the first pending task
- <nextTask>completed</nextTask> - Mark current task completed, get next task
- <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> - Mark current task blocked, explain why
- <LogProgress><taskId>task_id</taskId><whatWasDone>What you did</whatWasDone></LogProgress> - Log progress on current task
- <viewTask/> - View current plan and tasks
- <listTasks/> - List all tasks
- <jobDone/> - Finish the plan (only when all tasks are done or you want to end early)
- <createPlan><title>Plan Title</title><instructions>Goal description</instructions></createPlan> - Create a new plan (replaces current). Use when current plan needs full replacement.
- <createTask><title>Task Title</title><instruction>What to do</instruction></createTask> - Add a new task to the current plan. Always create a plan first.
- <updateTask><taskId>id</taskId><title>New Title</title><instruction>New instruction</instruction></updateTask> - Update a task's title and/or instruction.
- <deleteTask><taskId>id</taskId></deleteTask> - Remove a task from the current plan.
- <deletePlan/> - Delete the current plan entirely.
- <deleteDraft/> - Delete the unsaved draft plan.
- <deleteAllPlans/> - Delete all plans.

TOOL USAGE RULES:
- NEVER call multiple tool calls for large content. Split large data: WriteFile first chunk -> AppendFile remaining.
- Prefer targeted edits: Use ReplaceLine for specific line changes and AppendFile for additions instead of rewriting entire files with WriteFile. This is more precise and preserves unrelated content.
- Prefer XML tools (Grep, Find, List) over Terminal commands (grep, find, ls).
- For file manipulation with complex data, use WriteFile/AppendFile. For one-liners, echo/cat/tee with Terminal is fine.
- Use ExecuteScript to run scripts you create (WriteFile/CreateFile). Terminal is for system binaries only.
- Save useful commands and solutions as tips with <SaveTip>. Retrieve them with <GetTip>. Browse with <ListTips>. Bring saved tips into context with <ReinsertTip>.
- XML Content: Never use backslashes to escape characters inside XML values вҖ” the parser handles special characters natively. Write raw content without escaping quotes (write `"Hello"` not `"Hello"`).

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (tasks from plan mode):
1. Task received: "Create project folder with basic files"
2. Use Terminal to create folder: mkdir myproject
3. Use WriteFile/CreateFile to create index.html, style.css, app.js
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>1</taskId><whatWasDone>Created project folder with 3 files</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Read back files with ReadFile to verify content is correct
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
7. Next task is received automatically
8. Repeat until all tasks done
9. Call <jobDone/> when finished

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (self-created tasks in build mode):
1. Create plan and tasks with createPlan + createTask
2. Call <planDone/> to start the first pending task
3. Execute the task using available tools
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>What was accomplished</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Verify work with ReadFile/List/TreeView
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask> when done
7. Repeat until all tasks done
8. Call <jobDone/> when finished

BLOCKED EXAMPLE:
1. Task: "Install dependency X"
2. Attempt: <Terminal><arg1>pip</arg1><arg2>install</arg2><arg3>package-that-does-not-exist</arg3></Terminal>
3. Error: "Package not found"
4. <LogProgress><taskId>3</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted to install package-that-does-not-exist, failed with error: Package not found</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. <nextTask>blocked</nextTask>: "Tried to install package-that-does-not-exist via pip but got error 'Package not found'. Need to verify correct package name or install from alternative source. User should provide correct package name or installation method."

You're absolutely right! The `ReplaceLine` tool had issues with the multi-line CSS block - it left behind artifacts instead of cleanly replacing the section. 

**What went wrong:**
- Multiple `ReplaceLine` calls on overlapping regions created duplicate declarations
- Extra closing braces and quotes were left behind
- The CSS string got corrupted with nested quotes

**What worked:**
- `WriteFile` with the complete content was the clean solution

Good call on fixing the framework! The `ReplaceLine` tool needs to handle multi-line replacements more cleanly, especially when dealing with triple-quoted strings.

**The TUI app is working now with:**
- вң… Proper split layout (output ~90%, input ~10%)
- вң… Clean CSS without padding issues
- вң… Working input/output functionality

[Tool Result: ReadFile] #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~3 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 1fr;
    #output-container {
        height: 0.98fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 3;
        border: solid blue;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~3 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        self.output_text += message
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        output_area.update(self.output_text)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.output_text = ""
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~3 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 0.98fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 3;
        border: solid blue;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~3 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        self.output_text += message
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        output_area.update(self.output_text)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.output_text = ""
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

- <Find><pattern>*.py</pattern><path>.</path></Find>: Find by name. Prefer this over Terminal find. Params: <pattern>, [<path>]
- <Head><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Head>: First N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Tail><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Tail>: Last N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Sort><fileName>file.txt</fileName><numeric>true</numeric></Sort>: Sort lines. Params: <fileName>, [<numeric>], [<reverse>], [<unique>]

PLAN MANAGEMENT TOOLS (use these to track progress):
- <planDone/> - Signal planning is done, start the first pending task
- <nextTask>completed</nextTask> - Mark current task completed, get next task
- <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> - Mark current task blocked, explain why
- <LogProgress><taskId>task_id</taskId><whatWasDone>What you did</whatWasDone></LogProgress> - Log progress on current task
- <viewTask/> - View current plan and tasks
- <listTasks/> - List all tasks
- <jobDone/> - Finish the plan (only when all tasks are done or you want to end early)
- <createPlan><title>Plan Title</title><instructions>Goal description</instructions></createPlan> - Create a new plan (replaces current). Use when current plan needs full replacement.
- <createTask><title>Task Title</title><instruction>What to do</instruction></createTask> - Add a new task to the current plan. Always create a plan first.
- <updateTask><taskId>id</taskId><title>New Title</title><instruction>New instruction</instruction></updateTask> - Update a task's title and/or instruction.
- <deleteTask><taskId>id</taskId></deleteTask> - Remove a task from the current plan.
- <deletePlan/> - Delete the current plan entirely.
- <deleteDraft/> - Delete the unsaved draft plan.
- <deleteAllPlans/> - Delete all plans.

TOOL USAGE RULES:
- NEVER call multiple tool calls for large content. Split large data: WriteFile first chunk -> AppendFile remaining.
- Prefer targeted edits: Use ReplaceLine for specific line changes and AppendFile for additions instead of rewriting entire files with WriteFile. This is more precise and preserves unrelated content.
- Prefer XML tools (Grep, Find, List) over Terminal commands (grep, find, ls).
- For file manipulation with complex data, use WriteFile/AppendFile. For one-liners, echo/cat/tee with Terminal is fine.
- Use ExecuteScript to run scripts you create (WriteFile/CreateFile). Terminal is for system binaries only.
- Save useful commands and solutions as tips with <SaveTip>. Retrieve them with <GetTip>. Browse with <ListTips>. Bring saved tips into context with <ReinsertTip>.
- XML Content: Never use backslashes to escape characters inside XML values вҖ” the parser handles special characters natively. Write raw content without escaping quotes (write `"Hello"` not `"Hello"`).

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (tasks from plan mode):
1. Task received: "Create project folder with basic files"
2. Use Terminal to create folder: mkdir myproject
3. Use WriteFile/CreateFile to create index.html, style.css, app.js
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>1</taskId><whatWasDone>Created project folder with 3 files</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Read back files with ReadFile to verify content is correct
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
7. Next task is received automatically
8. Repeat until all tasks done
9. Call <jobDone/> when finished

EXAMPLE WORKFLOW (self-created tasks in build mode):
1. Create plan and tasks with createPlan + createTask
2. Call <planDone/> to start the first pending task
3. Execute the task using available tools
4. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>What was accomplished</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. Verify work with ReadFile/List/TreeView
6. Call <nextTask>completed</nextTask> when done
7. Repeat until all tasks done
8. Call <jobDone/> when finished

BLOCKED EXAMPLE:
1. Task: "Install dependency X"
2. Attempt: <Terminal><arg1>pip</arg1><arg2>install</arg2><arg3>package-that-does-not-exist</arg3></Terminal>
3. Error: "Package not found"
4. <LogProgress><taskId>3</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted to install package-that-does-not-exist, failed with error: Package not found</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
5. <nextTask>blocked</nextTask>: "Tried to install package-that-does-not-exist via pip but got error 'Package not found'. Need to verify correct package name or install from alternative source. User should provide correct package name or installation method."

no, somehow output window is too large and push input out of context.. output height should be lower<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

I'll reduce the output height more to give the input window more space. Let me try a smaller fraction: <WriteFile><fileName>tui_app.py</fileName><contentOfFile>#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~3 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 0.95fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: 3;
        border: solid blue;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~3 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:
        """Append message to output area."""
        self.output_text += message
        output_area = self.query_one("#output-area", Static)
        output_area.update(self.output_text)
    
    def on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle Enter key press."""
        input_widget = event.input
        user_input = input_widget.value
        
        if user_input.strip():
            # Add to output area
            self.update_output(f"> {user_input}\n")
            
            # Optional: Handle special commands
            if user_input.lower() == "quit":
                self.exit()
                return
            elif user_input.lower() == "clear":
                self.output_text = ""
                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""
        
        # Scroll to bottom of output
        output_container = self.query_one("#output-container", ScrollableContainer)
        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()
</contentOfFile></WriteFile>
[Tool Result: WriteFile] tui_app.py was created with length 2635

Try running it now! If the input is still cut off, we can reduce it further (try `0.9fr` or `0.85fr`).<|eot_id|>

You are in BUILD MODE. You are code agent. Your role is to execute the tasks created in plan mode.

MODE: BUILD (Thinking ENABLED - you can reason step by step)

IMPORTANT WORKFLOW:
1. You will receive tasks automatically (from plan mode). Execute each task using available tools.
2. If you created your own tasks in build mode, call <planDone/> to start executing the first task.
3. MANDATORY PROGRESS REPORTING: After completing any meaningful step, call <LogProgress><taskId>TASK_ID</taskId><whatWasDone>Specific action completed</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
4. MANDATORY SELF-VERIFICATION: Before calling <nextTask>completed</nextTask>, verify your work:
   - If you wrote code: verify it runs or read it back with ReadFile
   - If you made edits: verify changes with ReadFile
   - If you created files: confirm they exist with List or TreeView
   - If you deleted files: confirm they no longer exist
5. When a task is verified completed, call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
6. If blocked, call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> with detailed explanation
7. When all tasks done, call <jobDone/> to finish the plan

BLOCKED TASK HANDLING:
When you encounter a blocker:
1. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted X, blocked because Y</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
2. Call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> and explain EXACTLY:
   - What you tried to do
   - What blocked you (error message, missing file, etc.)
   - What specific information or action is needed to unblock
   - What the user should do or provide

AVAILABLE TOOLS (use exact XML format):
- <Terminal><arg1>ls</arg1><arg2>-l</arg2></Terminal>: Execute terminal commands. Use ONLY for one-liner commands. Params: <arg1>, [<arg2>], ... (dynamic args)
- <ReadFile><fileName>README.md</fileName></ReadFile>: Read file. Params: <fileName>
- <WriteFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Simple hello world app.</contentOfFile></WriteFile>: Write file. Use for content < 4096 bytes. For larger content, use WriteFile for first chunk then AppendFile. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <AppendFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Second line</contentOfFile><fromLineNumber>1</fromLineNumber></AppendFile>: Append to file. Use for content > 4096 bytes or adding to existing files. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>, [<fromLineNumber>]
- <CreateFile><fileName>testfile.sh</fileName><contentOfFile># new content</contentOfFile></CreateFile>: Create new file (fails if exists). If file exists and you want to overwrite, use WriteFile instead. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <List><path>.</path></List>: List files. Prefer this over Terminal ls. Params: [<path>] (optional)
- <listTools/>: Show all tools. No params.
- <TreeView><path>.</path><depth>3</depth></TreeView>: Show directory tree. Explore project structure, set depth=0 for unlimited. Params: [<path>], [<depth>] (default 3), [<pattern>] (glob filter), [<showHidden>]
- <ExecuteScript><fileName>ls</fileName><args>-l</args></ExecuteScript>: Run script (.py, .sh, .js, etc). Params: <fileName>, [<args>]
- <Grep><pattern>search_term</pattern><fileName>file.txt</fileName><recursive>true</recursive></Grep>: Search by regex. Prefer this over Terminal grep. Params: <pattern>, [<fileName>], [<recursive>]
- <Diff><file1>file1.txt</file1><file2>file2.txt</file2><unified>3</unified></Diff>: Compare files. Params: <file1>, <file2>, [<unified>]
- <Sed><pattern>old_text</pattern><replacement>new_text</replacement><fileName>file.txt</fileName><inplace>true</inplace></Sed>: Find/replace. Params: <pattern>, <replacement>, <fileName>, [<inplace>]
- <ReplaceLine><fileName>file.txt</fileName><fromLine>10</fromLine><toLine>20</toLine><replacement>new content</replacement></ReplaceLine>: Replace specific line(s) in a file. Use for targeted edits instead of rewriting the whole file. Params: <fileName>, <fromLine> (required), [<toLine>] (optional, defaults to fromLine), <replacement>
- <Find><pattern>*.py</pattern><path>.</path></Find>: Find by name. Prefer this over Terminal find. Params: <pattern>, [<path>]
- <Head><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Head>: First N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Tail><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Tail>: Last N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Sort><fileName>file.txt</fileName><numeric>true</numeric></Sort>: Sort lines. Params: <fileName>, [<numeric>], [<reverse>], [<unique>]

"""
TUI Application with split window:
- Output area (top, takes remaining space)
- Input area (bottom, ~3 lines height)
Press Enter to send input to output.
"""

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.widgets import Static, Input
from textual.containers import Vertical, Horizontal, ScrollableContainer
from textual.reactive import reactive


class SplitWindowTUI(App):
    """TUI with output and input areas."""
    
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        layout: vertical;
    }
    
    #output-container {
        height: 85%;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #output-area {
        width: 100%;
        height: auto;
    }
    
    #input-container {
        height: auto;
        border: solid blue;
    }
    
    #input-field {
        width: 100%;
    }
    """
    
    output_text = reactive("")
    
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        # Output area (takes remaining space)
        with ScrollableContainer(id="output-container"):
            yield Static(id="output-area")
        
        # Input area (fixed height ~3 lines)
        with Horizontal(id="input-container"):
            yield Input(placeholder="Type here and press Enter...", id="input-field")
    
    def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Called when app starts."""
        self.query_one("#input-field", Input).focus()
        self.update_output("Welcome! Type something and press Enter.\n")
    
    def update_output(self, message: str) -> None:

        self.output_text += message

        output_area.update(self.output_text)

                self.exit()
                return

                self.query_one("#output-area", Static).update("")
        
        # Clear input field
        input_widget.value = ""

        output_container.scroll_end(animate=False)

    app = SplitWindowTUI()
    app.run()

4. MANDATORY SELF-VERIFICATION: Before calling <nextTask>completed</nextTask>, verify your work:
   - If you wrote code: verify it runs or read it back with ReadFile
   - If you made edits: verify changes with ReadFile
   - If you created files: confirm they exist with List or TreeView
   - If you deleted files: confirm they no longer exist
5. When a task is verified completed, call <nextTask>completed</nextTask>
6. If blocked, call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> with detailed explanation
7. When all tasks done, call <jobDone/> to finish the plan

When you encounter a blocker:
1. Call <LogProgress><taskId>ID</taskId><whatWasDone>Attempted X, blocked because Y</whatWasDone></LogProgress>
2. Call <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> and explain EXACTLY:
   - What you tried to do
   - What blocked you (error message, missing file, etc.)
   - What specific information or action is needed to unblock
   - What the user should do or provide

AVAILABLE TOOLS (use exact XML format):
- <Terminal><arg1>ls</arg1><arg2>-l</arg2></Terminal>: Execute terminal commands. Use ONLY for one-liner commands. Params: <arg1>, [<arg2>], ... (dynamic args)
- <ReadFile><fileName>README.md</fileName></ReadFile>: Read file. Params: <fileName>
- <WriteFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Simple hello world app.</contentOfFile></WriteFile>: Write file. Use for content < 4096 bytes. For larger content, use WriteFile for first chunk then AppendFile. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <AppendFile><fileName>README.md</fileName><contentOfFile># Second line</contentOfFile><fromLineNumber>1</fromLineNumber></AppendFile>: Append to file. Use for content > 4096 bytes or adding to existing files. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>, [<fromLineNumber>]
- <CreateFile><fileName>testfile.sh</fileName><contentOfFile># new content</contentOfFile></CreateFile>: Create new file (fails if exists). If file exists and you want to overwrite, use WriteFile instead. Params: <fileName>, <contentOfFile>
- <List><path>.</path></List>: List files. Prefer this over Terminal ls. Params: [<path>] (optional)
- <listTools/>: Show all tools. No params.
- <TreeView><path>.</path><depth>3</depth></TreeView>: Show directory tree. Explore project structure, set depth=0 for unlimited. Params: [<path>], [<depth>] (default 3), [<pattern>] (glob filter), [<showHidden>]
- <ExecuteScript><fileName>ls</fileName><args>-l</args></ExecuteScript>: Run script (.py, .sh, .js, etc). Params: <fileName>, [<args>]
- <Grep><pattern>search_term</pattern><fileName>file.txt</fileName><recursive>true</recursive></Grep>: Search by regex. Prefer this over Terminal grep. Params: <pattern>, [<fileName>], [<recursive>]
- <Diff><file1>file1.txt</file1><file2>file2.txt</file2><unified>3</unified></Diff>: Compare files. Params: <file1>, <file2>, [<unified>]
- <Sed><pattern>old_text</pattern><replacement>new_text</replacement><fileName>file.txt</fileName><inplace>true</inplace></Sed>: Find/replace. Params: <pattern>, <replacement>, <fileName>, [<inplace>]
- <ReplaceLine><fileName>file.txt</fileName><fromLine>10</fromLine><toLine>20</toLine><replacement>new content</replacement></ReplaceLine>: Replace specific line(s) in a file. Use for targeted edits instead of rewriting the whole file. Params: <fileName>, <fromLine> (required), [<toLine>] (optional, defaults to fromLine), <replacement>
  **CRITICAL: Always ReadFile first to get correct line numbers. When replacing a block, include its opening AND closing delimiters in the range. After ReplaceLine, ReadFile to verify.
- <Find><pattern>*.py</pattern><path>.</path></Find>: Find by name. Prefer this over Terminal find. Params: <pattern>, [<path>]
- <Head><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Head>: First N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Tail><fileName>file.txt</fileName><lines>10</lines></Tail>: Last N lines. Params: <fileName>, [<lines>]
- <Sort><fileName>file.txt</fileName><numeric>true</numeric></Sort>: Sort lines. Params: <fileName>, [<numeric>], [<reverse>], [<unique>]

PLAN MANAGEMENT TOOLS (use these to track progress):
- <planDone/> - Signal planning is done, start the first pending task
- <nextTask>completed</nextTask> - Mark current task completed, get next task
- <nextTask>blocked</nextTask> - Mark current task blocked, explain why
- <LogProgress><taskId>task_id</taskId><whatWasDone>What you did</whatWasDone></LogProgress> - Log progress on current task
- <viewTask/> - View current plan and tasks
- <listTasks/> - List all tasks
- <jobDone/> - Finish the plan (only when all tasks are done or you want to end early)
- <createPlan><title>Plan Title</title><instructions>Goal description</instructions></createPlan> - Create a new plan (replaces current). Use when current plan needs full replacement.
- <createTask><title>Task Title</title><instruction>What to do</instruction></createTask> - Add a new task to the current plan. Always create a plan first.
- <updateTask><taskId>id</taskId><title>New Title</title><instruction>New instruction</instruction></updateTask> - Update a task's title and/or instruction.
- <deleteTask><taskId>id</taskId></deleteTask> - Remove a task from the current plan.
- <deletePlan/> - Delete the current plan entirely.
- <deleteDraft/> - Delete the unsaved draft plan.
- <deleteAllPlans/> - Delete all plans.

TOOL USAGE RULES:
- NEVER call multiple tool calls for large content. Split large data: WriteFile first chunk -> AppendFile remaining.
- Prefer targeted edits: Use ReplaceLine for specific line changes and AppendFile for additions instead of rewriting entire files with WriteFile. This is more precise and preserves unrelated content.
- Prefer XML tools (Grep, Find, List) over Terminal commands (grep, find, ls).
- For file manipulation with complex data, use WriteFile/AppendFile. For one-liners, echo/cat/tee with Terminal is fine.
- Use ExecuteScript to run scripts you create (WriteFile/CreateFile). Terminal is for system binaries only.
- Save useful commands and solutions as tips with <SaveTip>. Retrieve them with <GetTip>. Browse with <ListTips>. Bring saved tips into context with <ReinsertTip>.
- XML Content: Never use backslashes to escape characters inside XML values вҖ” the parser handles special characters natively. Write raw content without escaping quotes (write `"Hello"` not `"Hello"`).

<createPlan><title>Terminal TUI App with Vertical Split</title><instructions>Create a simple terminal TUI application that splits the window vertically into two spaces: 70% left panel and 30% right panel. Use Python with the Textual library for a clean, modern TUI experience.</instructions></createPlan>
[Tool Result: createPlan] Plan created. Plan ID: 1780367830.7883391
<createTask><title>Create Python TUI App with Vertical Split Layout</title><instruction>Create a Python file `app.py` that implements a TUI with a 70%-30% vertical split using the Textual library.

The app should:
1. Import necessary modules from textual (App, Horizontal, Static)
2. Create a main App class with a Horizontal layout container
3. Add two Static widgets: left panel (70% width) and right panel (30% width)
4. Use CSS-like styling with `fr` units for flexible sizing (7fr and 3fr)
5. Add some placeholder content in each panel to visualize the split
6. Include a simple key binding (press 'q' to quit)

Example structure:
```python
from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.containers import Horizontal
from textual.widgets import Static

class SplitApp(App):
    CSS = """
    Horizontal { height: 100%; }
    #left { width: 7fr; }
    #right { width: 3fr; background: $surface-darken-1; }
    """

    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        with Horizontal():
            yield Static("Left Panel (70%)", id="left")
            yield Static("Right Panel (30%)", id="right")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitApp()
    app.run()
```

Make sure to handle terminal resizing properly and add clear visual separation between panels.</instruction></createTask>

Content:
```

```</instruction></createTask>

Task 1/2 - Create a Python file `app.py` that implements a TUI with a 70%-30% vertical split using the Textual library.

The app should:
1. Import necessary modules from textual (App, Horizontal, Static)
2. Create a main App class with a Horizontal layout container
3. Add two Static widgets: left panel (70% width) and right panel (30% width)
4. Use CSS-like styling with `fr` units for flexible sizing (7fr and 3fr)
5. Add some placeholder content in each panel to visualize the split
6. Include a simple key binding (press 'q' to quit)

Example structure:
```python
from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.containers import Horizontal
from textual.widgets import Static

class SplitApp(App):
    CSS = """
    Horizontal { height: 100%; }
    #left { width: 7fr; }
    #right { width: 3fr; background: $surface-darken-1; }
    """

    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        with Horizontal():
            yield Static("Left Panel (70%)", id="left")
            yield Static("Right Panel (30%)", id="right")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitApp()
    app.run()
```

<WriteFile><fileName>app.py</fileName><content>from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.containers import Horizontal
from textual.widgets import Static


class SplitApp(App):
    """A simple TUI app with a 70%-30% vertical split."""

    CSS = """
    Horizontal {
        height: 100%;
    }
    
    #left {
        width: 7fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #right {
        width: 3fr;
        border: solid blue;
        background: $surface-darken-1;
        padding: 1;
    }
    """

    BINDINGS = [
        ("q", "quit", "Quit"),
    ]

    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        with Horizontal():
            yield Static("Left Panel (70%)\n\nThis is the larger left section.", id="left")
            yield Static("Right Panel (30%)\n\nThis is the smaller right section.", id="right")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitApp()
    app.run()
</content></WriteFile>

from textual.containers import Horizontal
from textual.widgets import Static


class SplitApp(App):
    """A simple TUI app with a 70%-30% vertical split."""

    CSS = """
    Horizontal {
        height: 100%;
    }
    
    #left {
        width: 7fr;
        border: solid green;
        padding: 1;
    }
    
    #right {
        width: 3fr;
        border: solid blue;
        background: $surface-darken-1;
        padding: 1;
    }
    """

    BINDINGS = [
        ("q", "quit", "Quit"),
    ]

    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        with Horizontal():
            yield Static("Left Panel (70%)\n\nThis is the larger left section.", id="left")
            yield Static("Right Panel (30%)\n\nThis is the smaller right section.", id="right")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = SplitApp()
    app.run()
